description_official: DRAFT — replace with verified text from NAVMC 1200.1E or current equivalent. The Rifleman is the foundation of the Marine Corps infantry squad and the basic scheme of maneuver for the ground-combat element.
description_plain: The rifleman is the Marine the rest of the Corps exists to support. You close with the enemy on foot, lead fire teams, and become an expert with the service rifle. This is ground combat — small units, long days, and the discipline that comes from doing the same hard things until they're automatic. Training pipeline runs through Parris Island or San Diego, then School of Infantry East or West. Most 0311s end up in an infantry battalion at Camp Pendleton, Camp Lejeune, or Kaneohe Bay. Expect deployments.
description_tldr: The Marine Corps rifleman is the ground-combat backbone of the USMC infantry — small-unit leader, marksman, and the Marine everything else supports.

asvab.raw_requirement: GT: 80 (DRAFT — verify against current Marine Corps classification directive)
asvab.notes: Verify against the current NAVMC 1200 series before publishing.

training_pipeline.location: Parris Island, SC or San Diego, CA
training_pipeline.description: Basic Marine training.

training_pipeline.location: Camp Geiger, NC or Camp Pendleton, CA
training_pipeline.description: DRAFT — verify current Infantry Marine Course duration.

compensation.typical_entry_rank: E-2 (Private First Class) on standard contract

special_pay.conditions: When deployed to a designated combat zone

common_duty_stations: Marine Corps Base Hawaii, Kaneohe Bay

standard_progression.notes: Entry rank if no promotion meritoriously earned in recruit training or shipping with no prior service benefit.
Most Marines ship or promote into this within the first year.
The workhorse rank of the Marine Corps. Most of your peers will be LCpls.
First NCO rank. Leads a 4-Marine fire team in combat.
Leads a 12–13 Marine infantry squad. Core small-unit leadership rank.
Most infantry Marines laterally move to 0369 Infantry Unit Leader at or before this rank to continue in the field.
Senior NCO — operational expert in the company.
Split track: 1stSgt leads Marines; MSgt is technical/staff expert.

typical_billets: Sergeant Major of a battalion, regiment, or higher
standard_progression.notes: Career capstone. SgtMaj is the senior enlisted leader of a unit.

b_billets.title: Recruiter
b_billets.description: Enlist new Marines. Three-year tour at a Recruiting Substation (RSS) in one of the Recruiting Stations across the country.
b_billets.eligibility: E-5 to E-7, meritorious record, Recruiter School (Basic Recruiter Course) graduate.
b_billets.typical_timeline: Mid-career, often after one or two operational tours.
b_billets.notes: Intense tour with high civilian exposure. Career-enhancing for promotion.

b_billets.title: Drill Instructor
b_billets.description: Train new Marines at Parris Island or San Diego. Arguably the toughest B-billet — long hours, high standards, constant scrutiny.
b_billets.eligibility: E-5 to E-7, meritorious record, Drill Instructor School graduate.

b_billets.title: Marine Security Guard (MSG)
b_billets.description: Guard U.S. embassies and consulates worldwide. Multiple postings across one tour, often at two or three different countries.
b_billets.eligibility: E-3 to E-7 (varies by posting), single or married without dependents for some assignments, MSG School graduate.
b_billets.typical_timeline: Open after first term; often first B-billet.
b_billets.notes: Unique billet — passports, protocol, and diplomatic posts. Verify current MSG program requirements before applying.

b_billets.title: Marine Combat Instructor (SOI)
b_billets.description: Train follow-on Marines at School of Infantry East or West. You teach the job you just did.
b_billets.eligibility: E-4 to E-7, SOI-approved record, Formal School Instructor Course.
b_billets.notes: Keeps you close to the infantry community.

b_billets.title: MARSOC Critical Skills Operator
b_billets.description: Marine Corps special operations. Requires Assessment & Selection followed by the Individual Training Course (ITC).
b_billets.eligibility: E-4 to E-7, meets MARSOC physical and record standards, passes A&S.
b_billets.typical_timeline: Usually attempted after a completed infantry tour.
b_billets.notes: High attrition at selection. This is a full reclassification rather than a temporary billet.

b_billets.title: Marine Corps Martial Arts Program Instructor-Trainer
b_billets.description: Oversee MCMAP training at a unit. Usually a collateral duty but can be a primary billet at training commands.
b_billets.eligibility: E-3 and above with MAI qualification.

lateral_moves.title: Scout Sniper
lateral_moves.description: Precision marksmanship and long-range reconnaissance. Lateral via Scout Sniper Basic Course.
lateral_moves.eligibility: 0311 qualified, high GT, passes selection course.
lateral_moves.notes: MOS status has been affected by Marine Corps Force Design restructuring — verify current status before committing to this path.

lateral_moves.title: Reconnaissance Marine
lateral_moves.description: Ground reconnaissance in support of MAGTF operations. Lateral via Basic Reconnaissance Course (BRC).
lateral_moves.eligibility: 0311 qualified, passes Reconnaissance Training Assessment Program (RTAP) and BRC.
lateral_moves.typical_timeline: After 1–2 years in the Fleet Marine Force.
lateral_moves.notes: High attrition at RTAP/BRC. Verify current pipeline.

lateral_moves.title: Machine Gunner
lateral_moves.description: Crew-served medium and heavy machine guns at the platoon and company level.
lateral_moves.eligibility: Typically assigned out of SOI rather than lateral moved — verify current NAVMC 1200 lateral-move policy.

lateral_moves.title: Mortarman
lateral_moves.description: 60mm and 81mm mortar crewman providing indirect fire support.
lateral_moves.eligibility: Typically assigned out of SOI rather than lateral moved — verify current NAVMC 1200 lateral-move policy.

lateral_moves.title: Antitank Missileman
lateral_moves.description: TOW, Javelin, and other antitank weapons systems.
lateral_moves.eligibility: Typically assigned out of SOI — verify current lateral-move policy.

reclass_options.title: Out-of-field reclassification
reclass_options.description: Marines can request reclassification to other MOS fields (admin, intel, logistics, comms, aviation support) at re-enlistment. Specific MOS availability depends on Marine Corps needs and the Marine's ASVAB line scores.
reclass_options.eligibility: Typically requires re-enlistment contract, classification package approval, and meeting ASVAB composite minimums for the target MOS.
reclass_options.typical_timeline: Requested during re-enlistment (end of first or second term).
reclass_options.notes: Not guaranteed. Approval depends on force-structure needs at the time of the request.

career_path.career_capstone_notes: Career infantry Marines typically lateral to 0369 Infantry Unit Leader at or before E-6 to remain in the infantry field at senior enlisted ranks. A smaller number commission as officers through Limited Duty Officer (LDO), Meritorious Commissioning Program (MCP), or the Enlisted Commissioning Program (ECP). Verify current program availability and eligibility before planning around any of these paths.

sources.name: NAVMC 1200.1E — Military Occupational Specialties Manual (DRAFT reference)

description_official: DRAFT — verify against current Marine Corps directive. Note: the Scout Sniper MOS has undergone restructuring in recent Marine Corps force design changes; verify current status before publishing.
description_plain: Scout snipers are the infantry's long-range eyes and precision-fire specialists. You earn this MOS by passing a selection course and Scout Sniper Basic Course — the washout rate is high and the training is hard on purpose. Status and force structure of this MOS have shifted with Force Design; confirm current standing before relying on anything here.
description_tldr: Marine Corps long-range reconnaissance and precision-fire specialist — status under review in recent Force Design changes.

physical_requirements.details: Enhanced physical screening for selection course; high-end PFT required.

description_plain: You run the platoon's heavy and medium machine guns. Think M240, M2 .50 cal, and the support-by-fire position in almost every infantry maneuver. Expect to carry weight and to be the first thing the enemy wants to silence.
description_tldr: Marine infantry machine gunner — crew-served weapons specialist providing suppressive and sustained fire.

physical_requirements.details: Standard USMC requirements; strong emphasis on load-bearing capacity.

training_pipeline.location: Parris Island, SC or San Diego, CA
Camp Geiger, NC or Camp Pendleton, CA

standard_progression.notes: Runs a pair of gun teams (M240 or M2) in the platoon's weapons section.
Leads the weapons squad: multiple gun teams plus rockets and assault assets depending on T/O.
Most 0331s lateral to 0369 at or before E-6 to continue in the infantry field.

career_path.career_capstone_notes: Career infantry machine gunners lateral to 0369 Infantry Unit Leader at or before E-6 to continue in the field. Commissioning via LDO, MCP, or ECP available to qualified Marines.

notes: DRAFT — verify all fields before publishing.

description_plain: The 0369 is the senior enlisted infantry leader — platoon sergeant, company gunnery sergeant, battalion-level billets. You get here by staying in, leading well, and proving you can run complex infantry operations at the platoon and company level.
description_tldr: Senior enlisted Marine Corps infantry leader — platoon sergeant and above; the promotion progression for career infantrymen.

asvab.notes: Promotion-progression MOS; not an entry-level assignment.

training_pipeline.location: Camp Lejeune, NC or Camp Pendleton, CA

b_billets.title: First Sergeant / Sergeant Major assignment

lateral_moves.description: MARSOC Critical Skills Operator via A&S + ITC.
lateral_moves.typical_timeline: Earlier in career; less common at senior E-levels.

reclass_options.eligibility: Varies by program; degree typically required for ECP/MCP.
reclass_options.notes: Verify current program eligibility and boards schedule.

career_path.career_capstone_notes: 0369 is the senior enlisted infantry track. Top billets are Battalion / Regimental / Division Sergeant Major and Marine Corps-level senior enlisted billets.

description_plain: Comms Marines. You set up and run the radio networks that infantry, logistics, and command elements depend on. Heavy emphasis on HF, VHF, UHF, and tactical satellite. Expect to be attached to infantry units as often as you're with a comms unit.
description_tldr: Marine Corps tactical radio operator — builds and runs voice and data networks for operational units.

training_pipeline.location: Parris Island or San Diego
Twentynine Palms, CA

standard_progression.notes: Career path diverges: continue as SME, or lateral to 0699 Telecommunications Chief.

career_path.career_capstone_notes: Senior comms Marines can serve at the Service-level staff. Civilian transition is strong — DoD COOL funds CompTIA A+, Network+, Security+, and Cisco certs, all of which translate directly to IT/telecom civilian roles.

description_plain: Artillery cannoneers man the M777 howitzer and related fire-support systems. You're part of a gun crew that puts rounds downrange in support of maneuver units. Physical job with crew drills you'll run thousands of times.
description_tldr: Marine Corps artillery crew member — operates howitzers to provide indirect fire support for ground combat.

physical_requirements.details: Standard USMC physical requirements; gun-crew work is physically demanding.

description_plain: Marine MP. You work base security, traffic enforcement, criminal investigation at the entry level, and can deploy for detainee operations or force protection. Strong translation to civilian law enforcement.
description_tldr: Marine Corps military police — base law enforcement, force protection, and deployed security operations.

physical_requirements.details: Must pass MP-specific physical standards and psychological screening.

description_plain: You drive the Marine Corps. MRAPs, 7-tons, MTVRs, HMMWVs, buses — convoy ops, logistics runs, troop movement. The skills transfer directly to a CDL in the civilian world, and the Marine Corps typically pays for the test through SkillBridge or DoD COOL.
description_tldr: Marine Corps motor vehicle operator — drives tactical and support vehicles; strong civilian CDL translation.

physical_requirements.details: Standard; must be medically qualified to operate military vehicles.

description_tldr: Marine Corps all-source intelligence analyst — supports tactical and operational commanders with intel products.

description_plain: You manage aviation parts and supply for a Marine aircraft wing. Inventory systems, parts ordering, receipt and issue, and the administrative side of keeping jets and helos flying. Office environment with squadron and hangar-deck work.
description_tldr: Marine Corps aviation supply specialist — manages parts and consumables for Marine aircraft squadrons.

training_pipeline.location: Parris Island, SC or San Diego, CA

description_plain: Mortar crewman. You run 60mm and 81mm mortars providing indirect fire to the rifle squads and platoons. Heavy tubes, heavy plates, lots of math on the fire-direction side. Arguably one of the most physically demanding jobs in the infantry.
description_tldr: Marine infantry mortarman — 60mm and 81mm indirect-fire specialist supporting maneuver units.

physical_requirements.details: Standard USMC requirements; heavy load-bearing focus.

description_plain: Recon Marines conduct ground reconnaissance, amphibious reconnaissance, and direct-action operations in support of MAGTF operations. Pipeline is brutal — RTAP, Basic Recon Course, and follow-on schools (jump, dive, survival). One of the most demanding non-MARSOC pipelines in the Marine Corps.
description_tldr: Marine Corps ground reconnaissance — tier-advanced infantry with amphibious, airborne, and direct-action training.

physical_requirements.details: High-end PFT, swim qualification through CWS-2, psychological screening, and passage of RTAP and BRC.

description_official: DRAFT — verify against current Marine Corps Officer MOS directives.
description_plain: The infantry officer leads Marines in ground combat. You commission as a 2nd Lieutenant, run The Basic School with every other new Marine officer, then attend Infantry Officer Course (IOC — the hardest initial officer pipeline in the Marine Corps). From there you lead a rifle platoon, then a weapons platoon, and by Captain you command a company.
description_tldr: Marine Corps infantry officer — leads rifle platoons and companies in ground combat operations.

asvab.raw_requirement: Officer commissioning — no enlisted ASVAB requirement. Bachelor's degree + OCS/NROTC/USNA.
asvab.notes: Officer accessions do not take the enlisted ASVAB. Standards are governed by officer selection boards.

physical_requirements.details: High-end PFT/CFT. Must pass The Basic School and Infantry Officer Course.

training_pipeline.location: Various
training_pipeline.description: Source of commission — Officer Candidate School (Quantico), Naval ROTC, U.S. Naval Academy, Platoon Leaders Course, or Enlisted Commissioning Program.

training_pipeline.location: Quantico, VA
training_pipeline.description: All Marine officers regardless of MOS attend TBS. Six months of infantry-officer-level training.

description_official: DRAFT — verify exact MOS code; the Marine Gunner has used different codes historically (2305, 0306). Verify current NAVMC 1200 series.
description_plain: The Marine Gunner is the infantry weapons subject-matter expert at the battalion and regimental level. You select as a Chief Warrant Officer from the senior enlisted infantry ranks (typically GySgt or MSgt). You wear a distinctive bursting-bomb insignia on your collar and you're the go-to expert on every weapons system the infantry uses.
description_tldr: Marine Corps infantry weapons warrant officer — the Marine Gunner; selected from senior enlisted infantry.

asvab.raw_requirement: Warrant Officer program — typically GT 110 plus board selection (DRAFT — verify)
asvab.notes: Warrant Officer accession is board-selected from the senior enlisted ranks; ASVAB is usually already on record.

physical_requirements.details: Must maintain Marine Corps fitness standards appropriate for the rank.

training_pipeline.location: Fleet assignment
training_pipeline.description: Candidates are typically GySgt or MSgt with a record of infantry expertise.
